steps.title: Use the slope to make a second point
steps.content: The graph passes through $(0,0)$. A slope of $39$ means one step right raises $y$ by $39$, so the next point is $(1,39)$. Enter those two points in Desmos.

steps.title: Fit the equation
steps.content: Run $y_1 \sim mx_1+b$. The regression gives $m=39$ and $b=0$, so $f(x)=39x$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice A has slope $-39$, choice B has slope $\frac{1}{39}$, and choice C has slope $1$ with intercept $-39$. Only choice D has slope $39$ and passes through the origin. Choice D is correct.
